title: podman 3.0rc2: podman build does not use the cache
body:
/kind bug
I'm on Debian unstable, and was using podman 3.0 rc1 for some time. After I upgraded to 3.0 rc2, cache does not work anymore with `podman build` (or with buildah 0.19.3, using `buildah bud`). If I revert back to rc1, it works again, even if the previous build was done with rc2. That is, rc2 is able to write to the cache, but to read from it.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

With rc2:

```
$ cat Containerfile 
──────┬───────────────────────────────
      │ File: Containerfile
──────┼───────────────────────────────
  1   │ FROM debian
  2   │ RUN sleep 3
──────┴───────────────────────────────
$ time podman build -t foobar .
STEP 1: FROM debian
STEP 2: RUN sleep 3
STEP 3: COMMIT foobar
--> 022b39fc015
022b39fc01563426e6bbea87c1ddce78bce725c35c4d5c9d5abcc940adbf0b61

real	0m4,579s
user	0m0,781s
sys	0m0,827s
$ time podman build -t foobar .
STEP 1: FROM debian
STEP 2: RUN sleep 3
--> Using cache 022b39fc01563426e6bbea87c1ddce78bce725c35c4d5c9d5abcc940adbf0b61
STEP 3: COMMIT foobar
--> 022b39fc015
022b39fc01563426e6bbea87c1ddce78bce725c35c4d5c9d5abcc940adbf0b61

real	0m3,553s
user	0m0,455s
sys	0m0,444s
```

Note that it says it's using the cache, but it's not (look at the time it took to run).

After this point, I revert back to rc1, and it is able to read the cache created by rc2 (and finished in 0s):

```
$ time podman build -t foobar .
STEP 1: FROM debian
STEP 2: RUN sleep 3
--> Using cache 022b39fc01563426e6bbea87c1ddce78bce725c35c4d5c9d5abcc940adbf0b61
STEP 3: COMMIT foobar
--> 022b39fc015
022b39fc01563426e6bbea87c1ddce78bce725c35c4d5c9d5abcc940adbf0b61

real	0m0,222s
user	0m0,134s
sys	0m0,093s
```


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.7
Built:        Wed Dec 31 21:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.18.0
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/bin/conmon'

    version: 'conmon version 2.0.25, commit: unknown'
  cpus: 4
  distribution:
    distribution: debian
    version: unknown
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: lemur
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.10.0-2-amd64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 2662879232
  memTotal: 33450237952
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: 'crun: /usr/bin/crun'
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.17
      commit: 0e9229ae34caaebcb86f1fde18de3acaf18c6d9a
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    exists: true
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: 'slirp4netns: /usr/bin/slirp4netns'
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.0.1
      commit: 6a7b16babc95b6a3056b33fb45b74a6f62262dd4
      libslirp: 4.4.0
  swapFree: 34238017536
  swapTotal: 34275848192
  uptime: 124h 13m 14.96s (Approximately 5.17 days)
registries: {}
store:
  configFile: /home/terceiro/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 6
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 6
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: 'fuse-overlayfs: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs'
      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.10.1
        fuse-overlayfs: version 1.4
        FUSE library version 3.10.1
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
  graphRoot: /home/terceiro/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:
    number: 97
  runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers
  volumePath: /home/terceiro/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes

  APIVersion: 2.0.0

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman/unstable 3.0.0~rc2+dfsg1-2 amd64 [upgradable from: 2.1.1+dfsg1-6]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

comments.body: no, wait, there is something wrong with my example there. I will re-test this.
I was misled by the message saying that it's using the cache. It says that, but it's not. I updated the issue description accordingly.

FWIW I cannot reproduce this with podman built from git and dependencies automatically managed by go, so this might be Debian-specific.

cc @siretart
I tried building podman against buildah 1.9.3 (which is the version in Debian) and then I can reproduce this.

I bisected this between buildah 1.19.2 and 1.19.3, and the first bad commit is

```
9b299588c0936376ef9ccd7938f1cef27c831418 is the first bad commit
commit 9b299588c0936376ef9ccd7938f1cef27c831418
Author: Urvashi Mohnani <umohnani@redhat.com>
Date:   Tue Jan 26 13:36:59 2021 -0500

    Rebuild layer if a change in ARG is detected
    
    Check whether the ARG in the containerfile is changed by
    either the --build-arg flag or local environment and use
    the cached layer or rebuild the layer accordingly.
    Add tests for this use case as well.
    
    Signed-off-by: Urvashi Mohnani <umohnani@redhat.com>

 imagebuildah/stage_executor.go  |  9 ++++++++-
 tests/bud.bats                  | 35 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-----
 tests/bud/build-arg/Dockerfile4 |  3 +++
 3 files changed, 41 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)
 create mode 100644 tests/bud/build-arg/Dockerfile4
```

Upstream's release version 3.0-rc2 does indeed build [against buildah 1.19.2](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/510a983a752a136a5df0bdbff9d14db0490956b2/go.mod#L13). However, [podman HEAD](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/aaa86c68c045519085fe7b174b6adce5f8373ad6/go.mod#L13) builds against buildah 1.19.3.

Did we find a regression ahead of time?

FWIW, if I bisect from the podman side, indeed the first bad commit is

```
commit 280f332bd90e5492ab67a3082013fa2707b34363
Author: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>
Date:   Fri Jan 29 11:57:20 2021 -0500

    Vendor in containers/buildah v1.19.3
    
    Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

 go.mod                                              |  2 +-
 go.sum                                              |  4 ++--
 vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/CHANGELOG.md   | 18 ++++++++++++++++++
 vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/buildah.go     |  2 +-
 vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/changelog.txt  | 18 ++++++++++++++++++
 vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/go.mod         |  8 ++++----
 vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/go.sum         | 17 +++++++++++++++++
 .../buildah/imagebuildah/stage_executor.go          |  9 ++++++++-
 vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/new.go         |  8 ++++----
 vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/util/types.go  |  2 +-
 vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/util/util.go   | 21 ++++++++++++++++++++-
 vendor/modules.txt                                  |  2 +-
 12 files changed, 95 insertions(+), 16 deletions(-)
```

@terceiro found the issue, opening a PR with the fix in buildah. Thanks for finding this! I was using the `using cache` output to determine if the cache was being used or not. Going to transfer this issue to buildah.
